items.title: Queensland premier learned of minister's AFP referral through media

children.content: Questions have been raised about how long Tim Mander waited to tell the Queensland premier about his referral to the AFP.

articles.title: Olympics minister stands aside after electoral commission makes referral to police

children.content: Queensland's Olympics Minister Tim Mander has stood aside from cabinet after he was referred to police by the Australian Electoral Commission.

gallery.altText: Premier david crisafulli in front of microphones

thumbnail.altText: Premier david crisafulli in front of microphones

avatar.alt: ABC Queensland state political reporter Jack McKay

items.title: Mother's love leads to cricket team representing Australia in the UK

children.content: A Queensland cricket team, started by a mother who wanted to give her autistic daughter a safe space to play, has just embarked on an international tour.

items.title: Farmers struggle to source popular cattle feed as dry conditions bite

children.content: A by-product of the cotton harvest, cottonseed boosts protein and fibre in animal diets. But as graziers ramp up their drought preparations, dry conditions are causing a supply slowdown.

image.altText: A handful of cottonseed in a mans hand.

items.title: The 600-kilometre trip to the skin specialist

children.content: Regional Queensland is facing a shortage of dermatologists, leaving some patients increasingly desperate.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits on a couch with hands clasped.
A woman in a crimson top exposes her bare elbow, which has dry skin.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a man with curly black hair and a moustache.

items.title: Four-metre waves on the way as south-east Queensland braces for beach erosion

children.content: Large swells driven by a Tasman Sea low are expected to bring hazardous surf and further erosion to already affected Gold Coast beaches over the weekend, before easing early next week.

image.altText: A 'no swimming' sign at Burleigh Heads, Gold Coast.

items.title: AI-generated resumes making hiring 'incredibly difficult', recruiters say

children.content: AI-perfected CVs are becoming the norm as more job applicants and employers use artificial intelligence, but recruiters say the technology could be making hiring trickier.

image.altText: A close up of text on a computer screen over a woman's shoulder.

items.title: Drivers allege Bruce Highway speed camera used for 'revenue raising'

children.content: The trailer-mounted speed camera has become a regular feature on the Bruce Highway, but these drivers claim they've been "unfairly" targeted in a blatant cash grab.

gallery.altText: A group of people standing staggered on the steps outside Bundaberg Magistrates Court.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people standing staggered on the steps outside Bundaberg Magistrates Court.

avatar.alt: A profile image of a young woman with blonde hair.

items.title: Rundown Rockhampton hotel sells for millions at court-ordered auction

children.content: After its closure in 2014, the formerly popular hotel became dilapidated and was frequented by squatters. It has now sold at a court-ordered auction for $5.75 million.

gallery.altText: Drone shot of multi storey concrete building with overgrown gardens, empty pool, smashed windows

thumbnail.altText: Drone shot of multi storey concrete building with overgrown gardens, empty pool, smashed windows

items.title: Woman accused of hit-and-run murder to stand trial

children.content: Larissa Rita Mae-Leigh Sant is accused of planning to kill her then-partner's ex-girlfriend, Kiesha Thompson, before striking her with a car while she was walking.

image.altText: CCTV shows the car moments before the alleged hit-and-run murder.

items.title: Woman dies after being hit by police car in Cairns

children.content: A 71-year-old woman has died after being hit by a police car in Cairns this morning.

image.altText: A police car parked at an angle off a road with police tape in foreground

items.title: Recycling contractor accused of having employees sleep on floor sues for $5m

children.content: Allegations that a depot operator in Queensland's container exchange recycling program conducted itself in a manner that could be likened to modern slavery have been detailed during a court hearing in a multi-million dollar federal court dispute.

image.altText: A birds eye view of green sheds.

items.title: Queensland inquiry wraps as submissions say child safety complaints were ignored

children.content: Final hearings have concluded at Queensland's inquiry into child safety, with a "lengthy" report expected to be delivered to the government on Friday.

image.altText: Two anonymous primary school-aged boys walk and balance on playground equipment in a park.

items.title: Deliberations begin in murder trial of farm worker who amputated pensioner's leg

children.content: A jury is deciding whether John Yalu, 41, committed murder when he used a circular saw to amputate the leg of a pensioner who had been "pressuring" him to remove the limb in exchange for $5,000.

gallery.altText: A sketch of a man in a court dock with a woman leaning in speaking to him

thumbnail.altText: A sketch of a man in a court dock with a woman leaning in speaking to him

avatar.alt: A profile image of a young man with brown eyes and dark hair.

items.title: Man with 'very serious' criminal history jailed over motorcyclist's death

children.content: The man had a "very high" level of methamphetamine in his blood when he crashed into the back of Ian Welldon's motorbike while failing to stop at a red light.

image.altText: A bald man with sunglasses on his head stares straight at the camera.

items.title: Whitsunday tour operators fear e-bike ban could destroy business

children.content: Guided tour operators say proposed laws banning under-16s from using personal mobility devices will put them out of businesses.

image.altText: A man holds a mountain bike over his head, standing on a platform overlooking a tropical island bay.

items.title: Earthquakes hit near regional Queensland town twice in four days

children.content: Experts say this week's seismic activity is unusual, but the region has a pattern of large and clustered earthquakes.

image.altText: The needle rests in the middle of the black lines indicating the strength of the quake on a red and white graph

items.title: NRL star Arrow reveals MND has already affected his speech

children.content: Jai Arrow reveals that MND has already affected his speech after announcing yesterday he was stepping away from the NRL after his shock diagnosis.

image.altText: Jai Arrow with his face in his hands at a sports press conference

items.title: Why the future of one of Queensland's last true fishing towns is on the brink

children.content: Karumba's rich fishing industry dates back to the roaring, prawning era of the 60s, but major blows are seeing a seafood decline. However, the Gulf of Carpentaria residents say they "wouldn't live anywhere else".

gallery.altText: Sunset over karumba beach, orange glow, with bird near sun in water

thumbnail.altText: Sunset over karumba beach, orange glow, with bird near sun in water

items.title: Hackers in an 'ethical battle' beyond cyber attacks and exploits

children.content: Student hackers compete in the International Cybersecurity Challenge 2026, an ethical hacking contest simulating real cyber attacks and defences with real-world consequences.

image.altText: MAN SMILING IN FRONT OF COMPUTER

items.title: Elderly woman dies 11 days after fatal crash on Queensland's Southern Downs

children.content: The woman has died of injuries sustained as a passenger in an SUV that allegedly swerved to avoid an oncoming car in the wrong lane on May 9.

image.altText: A night time photo of a Queensland Police Service car with its blue light illuminated.

items.title: Meet the staff and volunteers who make Meals on Wheels go round

children.content: For 70 years, Meals on Wheels has been serving Ipswich residents, and every meal comes with a welfare check and a conversation.

gallery.altText: An elderly couple show off their meals in disposable aluminium trays
A black and white photo showing two women in a kitchen serving meals.
A man stands leaning against the back of a car with the boot open smiling.

thumbnail.altText: An elderly couple show off their meals in disposable aluminium trays
A black and white photo showing two women in a kitchen serving meals.
A man stands leaning against the back of a car with the boot open smiling.
